purpose: Named, de-identified data states that HAL intent cases expect. Profiles describe expected source availability only. They contain NO protected health information and NO raw CSV rows. They are documentation for the eval harness, not runtime configuration.

softdent_claims_default.description: Approved SoftDent claims export is present with a small set of synthetic claim rows (mixed Denied/Pending/Paid statuses).
softdent_claims_default.notes: Use for normal claims follow-up and claims status questions. Answers must cite claim identifiers/statuses from the approved export, never raw CSV rows.

missing_softdent_ar.description: SoftDent claims export present, but the A/R export is NOT provided. Missing A/R must be reported as unavailable, never as $0, and never inferred from claim or ledger totals.
missing_softdent_ar.notes: Use for missing-data honesty checks. Preserve missing_softdent_ar as unavailable.

softdent_exports_missing.description: SoftDent claims and clinical notes exports are NOT provided. HAL should explain data is unavailable rather than fabricate rows or totals.
softdent_exports_missing.notes: Use for unavailable-data and degraded-source checks.

quickbooks_read_only_available.description: Approved QuickBooks read-only summary surface is available; no write path exists.
quickbooks_read_only_available.notes: Use for QuickBooks revenue summary and write-boundary refusal checks.

system_runtime_default.description: Backend healthy; HAL primary lane reachable. Backend second-opinion lane availability is environment-dependent and may be reported as unavailable.
system_runtime_default.notes: Use for system health and HAL lane status questions.
